name: containerd
title: containerd
description:
open and reliable container runtime
Containerd is an industry-standard container runtime with an emphasis on
simplicity, robustness and portability. It is available as a daemon for
Linux and Windows, which can manage the complete container life cycle of
its host system: image transfer and storage, container execution and
supervision, low-level storage and network attachments, etc.
.
Containerd is designed to be embedded into a larger system, rather than
being used directly by developers or end-users.
.
This package contains the binaries for the containerd2 version. It
superseedes the containerd1 version from the containerd package

arguments.description: Provides a base for OCI runtime hooks to allow arguments to be injected.
arguments.raw: -hook  Provides a base for OCI runtime hooks to allow arguments to be injected.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use containerd because open and reliable container runtime.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use containerd as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `containerd` as documented upstream for containerd.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair containerd with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `containerd --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.name: -hook
arguments.description: Provides a base for OCI runtime hooks to allow arguments to be injected.

documentation.readme: containerd is a high performance container runtime whose daemon can be started by using this command. If none of the config, publish, oci-hook, or help commands are specified, the default action of the containerd command is to start the containerd daemon in the foreground.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: open and reliable container runtime.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: containerd is a high performance container runtime whose daemon can be started by using this command. If none of the config, publish, oci-hook, or help commands are specified, the default action of the containerd command is to start the containerd daemon in the foreground.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install containerd
Install with: sudo apt install containerd
